item.title: Rains likely in parts of country as monsoon approaches
item.description: Rain accompanied by temporary gusty winds and lightning flashes may drench parts of Bangladesh in 24 hours from 9:00am on Monday, according to the Bangladesh Meteorological Department....

item.title: Arakan Army detains 7 Bangladeshi fishermen off Teknaf
item.description: Seven Bangladeshi fishermen were taken away along with two fishing trawlers by Myanmar’s armed group Arakan Army while they were fishing in the Bay of Bengal and the Naf River estuary at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Monday morning....

item.title: Pvt sector credit growth nears 5pc
item.description: Private sector credit growth in Bangladesh edged up to 4.98 per cent in May from 4.72 per cent in March, but remained near a record low, reflecting continued weakness in business investment and bank lending......

item.title: Biman set to resume Dhaka-Narita flight on July 27
item.description: Biman Bangladesh Airlines is set to resume direct flights to Narita in Japan from July 27....

item.title: Myanmar president visits China
item.description: Myanmar’s coup-commander turned president Min Aung Hlaing touched down in China on Monday for talks with Xi Jinping, his first visit since taking over as civilian leader after Beijing-backed elections rejected by democracy monitors....

item.title: FY27 budget sensitive towards disadvantaged: Debapriya
item.description: The proposed national budget for the financial year 2026-27 reflected a degree of policy ‘sensitivity’ toward low-income, marginalised and disadvantaged groups, but concerns remain over its weak framework and implementation capacity, said economist Debapriya Bhattacharya on Monday......

item.title: Budget success hinges on reforms, better business climate: experts
item.description: Economists and business leaders on Sunday said the success of the proposed FY2026-27 budget hinges on effective implementation, institutional reforms and a better business environment, warning that growth and revenue targets may be hard to achieve without stronger governance and competitiveness......

item.title: Singer, ex-AL MP Momtaz gets bail in 3 cases
item.description: The High Court on Monday granted bail to former Awami League lawmaker and renowned folk artiste Momtaz Begum in three separate cases filed with Ashulia and Mirpur police stations....

item.title: BB support for Islami Bank rises to Tk 5,000cr
item.description: Bangladesh Bank on Monday provided another Tk 2,500 crore in emergency liquidity support to Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC and took direct control of its board following days of heavy deposit withdrawals and growing liquidity pressure......

item.title: Net RMG exports $5.64b in Q3
item.description: Net exports of readymade garment items stood at $5.64 billion in the January-March or third quarter of the current financial year 2025-26, according to Bangladesh Bank data......

item.title: WB emphasises research to boost agri-productivity
item.description: Bangladesh should shift agricultural public spending from broad fertiliser and rice support to research, irrigation, market access, climate resilience, and farmer services to create a more productive and diversified agrifood system, a World Bank report suggested......

item.title: Court denies bail to Abul Barkat in July murder case
item.description: A Dhaka metropolitan magistrate’s court on Monday rejected the bail petition of former Janata Bank chairman professor Dr Abul Barkat in a case filed over the killing of businessman Abdul Wadud during the July uprising in the capital’s Nilkhet area....

item.title: REHAB opposes 15pc gain tax on landowners’ flats
item.description: The Real Estate and Housing Association of Bangladesh on Monday opposed the proposed 15 per cent capital gain tax on flats received by landowners under joint development agreements and demanded the withdrawal of the measure, saying that it would increase housing costs and deepen the sector’s ongoing difficulties......

item.title: BB issues new guideline on B2C online export
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has issued new guidelines expanding the framework for Business-to-Consumer (B2C) exports through international online marketplaces, aiming to simplify procedures and enhance exporters’ access to global digital trade platforms......

item.title: Experts welcome tax reforms in FY27 budget
item.description: Rahman Rahman Huq, chartered accountants, organised a technical seminar on the Finance Bill 2026, bringing together senior executives and finance leaders from multinational corporations, foreign investors, large local business groups, banks, financial institutions and other corporate entities......

item.title: Govt to make impact assessment of family card programme
item.description: The government would conduct impact assessment of the Family Card on the life standard of beneficiaries, said officials on Monday....

item.title: Gold stages consecutive price leaps
item.description: Gold prices in Bangladesh have surged by Tk 12,072 per bhari over two consecutive days, reversing a prolonged declining trend, as the Bangladesh Jewellers Association (BAJUS) cited rising pure gold prices in the local market......

item.title: Pubali Bank honours Everest conqueror Nurunnahar Nimni
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC honoured its principal officer and mountaineer Nurunnahar Nimni for conquering Mount Everest, the highest peak in the world......

item.title: PM seeks media support in building humane state
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday sought the cooperation from the media in building a humane state and fostering human values among people....

item.title: NRB Bank launches Visa prepaid payroll card
item.description: NRB Bank PLC, in collaboration with Visa, has recently launched Bangladesh’s first insurance-embedded Visa prepaid payroll card......

item.title: Karnaphuli Insurance settles Incepta’s claim
Tk 56,117 crore supplementary budget passed
item.description: The Jatiya Sangsad on Monday approved a supplementary budget of Tk 56,117.59 crore for the 2025–26 financial year, authorising additional expenditure by various ministries and divisions while simultaneously reducing overall government spending....

item.title: UCB gets BB recognition for job creation
item.description: United Commercial Bank PLC has been honoured with a certificate of appreciation by the Bangladesh Bank in recognition of its significant contribution to Supporting Post Covid-19 and Small Scale Employment Creation Project, supported by the Asian Development Bank......

item.title: Argentina begin title defence against confident Algeria
item.description: Argentina start their bid for back-to-back World Cups on Tuesday with a side full ‌of familiar faces from Qatar 2022, facing an Algerian team buoyed by warm-up wins and featuring a 20-year-old rising star nicknamed after Diego Maradona....

item.title: SBAC Bank holds 13th AGM
item.description: SBAC Bank PLC on Monday held its 13th annual general meeting at RAOWA Convention Centre in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Beximco Pharma starts supplying low-cost cystic fibrosis medicine
item.description: Beximco Pharmaceuticals PLC on Monday began supplying a low-cost generic version of a breakthrough cystic fibrosis medicine to patients from six countries, claiming to be the first company to make an affordable alternative to the treatment available globally......

item.title: Citizens Bank holds 5th AGM
item.description: Citizens Bank PLC on Sunday held the 5th annual general meeting at its head office in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Toothless Spain held by debutants Cape Verde
item.description:
Spain's ghosts of recent World Cup horror shows reappeared in Atlanta as the European champions were held 0-0 by debutants Cape Verde in their opening game on Monday.
...

item.title: Ad-din asks health minister to prove bribery allegation
item.description: The Ad-din Medical College Hospital authorities on Monday challenged health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain to prove his allegation that they had attempted to bribe him following the deaths of six newborns at the hospital....

item.title: July uprising a civilian movement, not armed campaign: ICT
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2, in its full verdict in the July 2024 quota movement’s first martyr Abu Sayeed murder case, observed that the July uprising was essentially a civilian movement and a mass public protest, not an armed or militant campaign....

item.title: Riad Mahmud Chy Riad joins Standard Islami Bank as MD
item.description: Riad Mahmud Chowdhury has joined Standard Islamic Bank PLC as managing director, a press release said......

item.title: Minister’s remark on university education misleading, cursory
item.description: FOR years, national education policies have suffered from the absence of a coherent, long-term vision. The education minister’s recent comments indicate that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government is more inclined to go with the wind than to undertake the policy shift needed to address the sector’s structural challenges. While addressing a workshop on the......

item.title: Quality education, not just access, is what matters
item.description: THE Education Sector Analysis 2026, presented by UNICEF, lays bare a learning crisis that threatens to undermine the country’s development ambitions. The findings are particularly alarming because they reveal that increased access to education has not translated into meaningful learning outcomes. Only half of children aged 7–14 possess foundational reading......

item.title: Where is the money?
item.description: THE proposed budget for the 2026–27 financial year is impressive by almost any numerical measures. At Tk 9.38 trillion, it is the largest budget in Bangladesh’s history. Allocations for education and health care have reached unprecedented levels. Social protection programmes have expanded. Tax relief has been offered on a range of essential commodities. Support has been promised for entrepreneurs, freelancers, exporters and vulnerable groups......

item.title: Rising heat, rising risks
item.description: EXTREME heat is rapidly becoming one of Bangladesh’s most serious but least acknowledged climate threats. In April 2024, temperatures in parts of the country crossed 42°C during one of the longest and most intense heatwaves on record, forcing school closures and disrupting daily life on a wide scale. The event made clear that extreme heat is no longer an occasional......

item.title: University of Dhaka and collective failures
item.description: THIS happened a decade ago. The chair of the department where I studied at the University of Dhaka in the early 1990s invited me, along with others, to an exchange of views on the Higher Education Quality Enhancement Project, officially HEQEP but jokingly ‘hiccup’ even to many university teachers. The University Grants Commission project, which the World Bank funded, was meant to upgrade university teaching, research and institutional facilities......

item.title: Birth of modern celebrity machine
item.description: MORE than 15 years after his death, Michael Jackson remains one of the most recognisable cultural figures on the planet. New artists continue to dominate charts, social media produces celebrities at unprecedented speed and digital platforms constantly reshape popular culture. Yet Jackson’s influence endures in ways that extend far beyond music. His title as the “King of Pop” survives because he fundamentally changed how entertainment is communicated, consumed and remembered......

item.title: No panic, says De la Fuente after Spain held by Cape Verde
item.description: Spain boss Luis de la Fuente said he is still convinced La Roja will go far at the World Cup despite failing to break down debutants Cape Verde in a 0-0 draw to open their tournament....

item.title: Belgium and Egypt draw 1-1 in World Cup group game
item.description: Belgium and Egypt drew 1-1 in their World Cup opener on Monday after a second-half Belgian equalizer spoiled Mohamed Salah's 34th birthday....

item.title: Cape Verde hero Vozinha sheds 'tears of resilience'
item.description: Spain failed to find a way past inspired Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha as the 40-year-old secured a stunning 0-0 draw for the World Cup debutants and was reduced to tears at his heroics....

item.title: Greed or player health? 'Damaging' drinks breaks under spotlight
item.description: Virgil van Dijk does not like them, fans have started booing them and the many critics say they are ruining the World Cup....

item.title: BMU holds seminar on AI in radiology
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University held a seminar titled Artificial Intelligence: A New Era in Radiology at the A Block Auditorium of BMU in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Juba Dal leader arrested for attempted rape, expelled from party
item.description: The police have arrested union-level Juba Dal leader Abul Kashem, alias Kashem Sheikh, for allegedly attempting to rape a seventh-grade schoolgirl at Ataikula in Pabna....

item.title: BUET hosts seminar on edn, research in AI era
item.description: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology’s Institute of Appropriate Technology organised a seminar titled Education, Research and Development in the AI Era at BUET in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Imamul Kabir Shanto’s death anniv observed
item.description: A commemorative event was organised marking the sixth anniversary of death of freedom fighter Md Imamul Kabir Shanto, founder chairman of Shanto-Mariam Foundation, Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology and Sundarban Courier Service Limited, at the......

item.title: Information minister for independent media commission
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Monday said that establishing an independent and responsible media commission based on an objective perspective was essential in the present context....

item.title: Banned BCL’s flash procession briefly halts Dhaka-Khulna highway
item.description: Activists of the banned Bangladesh Chhatra League, the student wing of the Awami League, briefly halting traffic on Monday after staging a flash procession on the Dhaka-Khulna highway at Kashiani upazila in Gopalganj, to protest the defacement of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman’s mural with ink....

item.title: Evicted Rakhines await rehabilitation, justice
item.description: The last six families of Chhoianipara, the Rakhine village that was evicted about five years ago in Patuakhali to make way for the Payra Port, have yet not been rehabilitated....

item.title: Three arrested over Jordan recruitment scam
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department of the police has arrested three members of an alleged fraud gang for using forged documents and false promises of jobs in Jordan....
